title: Cosmos Remix — Double Helix you can retune and reskin live
summary: Remixable particle double helix (DNA): two intertwined sine strands twist up a vertical axis with cross-rungs, parametrised by i along the helix and rotating with time, while depth (front/back of the rotating tube) maps to particle size and brightness for a 3D feel. A live glass panel retunes palette, shape, density, speed, dot size and a Twists knob in real time, and reskins to any brand colour in one click. Self-contained, zero deps; ?auto=1 hides the panel and renders a deterministic seamless loop.
usage_notes: RUN: open index.html and use the panel (top-right) to retune live — pick a palette to reskin to any brand colour, drag Density/Spread/Speed/Dot, set Twists to add helix turns, press the Seed button to shuffle, Copy to copy your config. KEY TECHNIQUES: a double helix is two sine strands 180 degrees apart in phase (ang = f*2pi*Twists + spin + phase) running along a vertical axis, with dotted cross-rungs linking them; z = sin(ang) is the depth that drives per-particle size and alpha for a pseudo-3D look; all motion is a pure function of params + time u (sin/cos with integer harmonics) so the panel updates instantly and the capture loop stays seamless. PARAMS convention: window.__params describes the knobs and window.setParam(key,value) applies them, so a generic remix harness can auto-build the panel for ANY effect; theme tokens = a PALETTES map driving both a CSS accent var and the dot ramp(cols,t); shapes come from the shared SHAPES set via drawShape(). CAPTURE: ?auto=1 hides panel + window.__seek(t). TUNE: raise Twists for a tighter coil, Density for more beads, Spread for a fatter tube; add palettes to PALETTES, extend window.__params.
